summary: Executed backend API regression (health/runs/share/ai tutor) and end-to-end UI validation for dashboard, sorting, graph, pathfinding, maze, theme switching, AI tutor drawer, and 1920x800 layout. Core requested flows are working.
action_items: Normalize sorting recommendation input_type values between frontend and backend (frontend sends 'nearly'/'few-unique' while backend checks 'nearly sorted'/'few unique').
critical_code_review_comments: Recommendation branch mismatch: backend conditional for input_type does not align with frontend preset values, causing recommendation quality degradation for partially sorted/few-unique datasets.
Graph and maze generators expose seed in API signatures/state but do not use deterministic seeded RNG, so replay reproducibility is limited.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_run_core_endpoint run record via /api/runs during API regression testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Baseline coverage now exists in /app/backend/tests/test_core_endpoints.py. UI flows for Sorting/Graph/Pathfinding/Maze and AI tutor were executed against public URL and passed after targeted retest for AI drawer interaction.
rca of the issue: RCA: Recommendation logic mismatch. Repro: On Sorting page choose preset 'Nearly Sorted' or 'Few Unique' and click Recommend; frontend sends input_type values ('nearly'/'few-unique') that do not satisfy backend condition expecting ('nearly sorted'/'few unique'), so intended recommendation branch is skipped. Mitigation: use normalized enum constants shared across frontend/backend or map preset values before request.
